economic_notes: First Nation has contractual rights to royalty fees of 1.25% to 3.5% of gross invoices from private sector entities operating a gold mine on First Nation land. Royalty fee recognized in current year: $1,486,110 (2023: $1,283,852). Capital contribution of $6,700,000 into Naagaaniiyang Limited Partnership for acquisition of Victoria Inn Hotel. Investment in Argonaut Gold Inc. (market value $1,452,296). Amount due from Bamkushwada Limited Partnership $1,500,000 interest-free.
notes: Audit is QUALIFIED due to non-adoption of PS 3280 Asset Retirement Obligations (effective April 1, 2022) - management has not completed assessment of tangible capital assets for potential asset retirement obligations, making it impossible to quantify the impact on expenses, surplus, and accumulated surplus for 2024 and 2023. Four entities fully consolidated: Michipicoten First Nation Cultural Association, Michipicoten First Nation Economic Development Corporation, Michipicoten Development Limited Partnership, and Michipicoten General Partnership. No schedule of remuneration for Chief, Council, or staff was included in the extracted text. Total government transfers calculated as ISC ($4,919,302) + Canada ($244,624) + Ontario ($664,466) + CMHC (included in long-term debt/housing) = $6,846,694 excluding Aboriginal organizations and LP distributions which are not government transfers. OSR includes Aboriginal organizations, trust income, rentals/interest/other. Budget year was adopted April 28, 2022 and not prepared on PSAS basis. Accumulated surplus per Statement of Accumulated Surplus is $52,592,348 but Statement of Operations shows $53,089,703 - difference of $497,355 relates to remeasurement losses recorded separately.
